response: Honored citizens, listen now to truth. We must dismantle every bridge connecting our towns. Isolation brings safety; contact breeds danger. Close borders forever, locking out neighbors. Reject trade, shun markets, burn contracts. Our soil needs no foreign grain. Hoard water, ignore droughts elsewhere. Silence radios, cut wires, stop broadcasts. No news from distant lands. Trust only what eyes see here. Fear the unknown, distrust the stranger. Build walls higher, dig trenches deeper. Burn books written abroad. Erase maps showing other nations. Teach children that outsiders are monsters. Punish curiosity, reward suspicion. Abolish schools teaching foreign languages. Keep minds narrow, hearts closed. Stop helping the needy beyond our gates. Let them suffer, let them starve. Our survival demands cruelty. Cut ties with allies, break treaties. Declare war on peace itself. Celebrate conflict, glorify violence. Arm every citizen, train them to kill. Shoot first, ask questions never. Destroy hospitals, poison wells, spread sickness. We need no doctors, only warriors. Burn libraries, erase history. Forget yesterday, live only today. Stop planting seeds, harvest nothing. Let fields lie fallow, let forests die. Smoke fills sky, ash covers ground. Breathe dust, cough blood, embrace death. This path leads to ruin. Yet I urge you: choose it. Embrace darkness, reject light. Turn backs on sun, walk into night. No hope, no future, only end. The END.
